1. News
  2. Entertainment

How to watch MGM+ shows, movies, and more on Prime Video

MGM+ offers exclusive original series for $7.99 a month through a Prime Video subscription, including acclaimed shows like Robin Hood, FROM, and Godfather of Harlem.

    Written by Tyler Greenawalt

    4 min read

    Key takeaways

    • MGM+ costs $7.99 a month or $61.99 a year with a seven-day free trial on Prime Video.
    • Original series include FROM, Godfather of Harlem, Robin Hood, The Institute, and the upcoming crime drama The Westies.
    • MGM+ is available through Prime Video Subscriptions, the MGM+ app, cable providers, and online.
    MGM+ is one of many amazing streaming services you can access directly through Prime Video Subscriptions.
    The service offers a lineup of exclusive and acclaimed original series and docuseries, recently released movies, and classic film franchises available on demand, online, and across devices. MGM+ is also available in more than 30 countries around the world.

    Watch the trailer for 'Verity,' an Amazon MGM Studios film premiering October 2

    The film adaptation of Colleen Hoover’s best-selling psychological thriller stars Anne Hathaway, Dakota Johnson, and Josh Hartnett.

    Here’s everything you need to know about MGM+, including the top new original series to watch on it.

    How do I sign up for MGM+?

    You can subscribe directly to MGM+ through your Prime Video account for $7.99 a month or $61.99 a year in the United States. That means you won’t need to download additional apps and no cable is required. You’ll be able to access all your content and manage your subscription directly from your Prime Video account, which makes for a smoother and easier experience.
    There is a seven-day free trial option as well to try out MGM+.
    Check your local Amazon website for pricing in your region.

    How else can I access MGM+?

    MGM+ is available online and through the MGM+ app, as well as through cable, satellite, and emerging digital distribution channels. You can sign up to stream MGM+ in the app or on a desktop, or add it to your existing service with another provider. Check out the full list of providers.

    What can I watch on MGM+?

    Customers can subscribe to MGM+ directly on Prime Video and watch their favorites without having to switch apps. MGM+ is also available nationwide through cable, telco, satellite, digital distribution channels, and the MGM+ app. The streaming service offers a broad lineup of original series and docuseries, as well as the latest movie releases and classic franchises—all ad-free and on demand.
    Here are some of the biggest original series you can stream now on MGM+, as well as a rundown of upcoming titles you can watch soon:

    ‘FROM’

    FROM unravels the mystery of a nightmarish town that traps all those who enter. As the unwilling residents fight to keep a sense of normalcy and search for a way out, they must also survive the threats of the surrounding forest—including the terrifying creatures that come out when the sun goes down.
    Cast: Harold Perrineau, Catalina Sandino Moreno, Hannah Cheramy, Simon Webster

    ‘Robin Hood’

    Robin Hood follows Robert of Locksley's journey from forester's son to outlaw leader. Rebellion ignites in Sherwood, intrigue builds, and a legend is born.
    Cast: Jack Patten, Lauren McQueen, Sean Bean, Lydia Peckham, Steven Waddington, Richard Charles Lintern, Ryan Gage, Tom Mison

    ‘The Institute’

    Joe Freeman as Luke Ellis in 'The Institute.'
    The Institute follows teenage genius Luke Ellis, who is abducted by a secret organization that takes kids with psychic powers for use in a mysterious project. The place is run by Ms. Sigsby, a sadistic true believer who engages Luke in a deadly battle of wills.
    Cast: Ben Barnes, Mary-Louise Parker

    ‘Vanished’

    In Vanished, Alice and her boyfriend Tom are enjoying a romantic trip to Paris when Tom suddenly disappears aboard a train heading to the south of France. Alice is then trust into a dangerous mystery that forces her to question everything she thought she knew about the man she loves.
    Cast: Kaley Cuoco, Sam Claflin

    ‘Godfather of Harlem’

    Forest Whitaker as Bumpy Johnson in 'Godfather of Harlem.'
    In Godfather of Harlem, Bumpy Johnson seeks to regain control of Harlem after returning from Alcatraz. With the streets controlled by the Italian mob, Bumpy must take on the Genovese crime family, led by Vincent "Chin" Gigante. To thwart the Italian's drug trade, Bumpy forms an alliance with Malcolm X.
    Cast: Forest Whitaker, Lucy Fry

    ‘Billy the Kid’

    Tom Blyth as Billy The Kid.
    Billy the Kid tells the life story of one of the most infamous outlaws from history—from his humble Irish roots and his early days as a cowboy and gunslinger in the American frontier, to his pivotal role in the Lincoln County War and beyond.
    Cast: Tom Blyth, Daniel Webber, Eileen O'Higgins

    ‘The Hillside Strangler’

    In this gripping series, The Hillside Strangler unmasks the truth behind Los Angeles' most terrifying serial killer case of the 1970s with interviews with convicted killer Ken Bianchi, officials, survivors, and rare archival recordings.
    Cast: Frank Salerno

    Coming soon to MGM+

    • The Westies
    • American Hostage
    • A Tale of Two Cities
    • Magnificent Seven
    • Treasure Island
    • Legacy of Spies
    For more information about the current MGM+ lineup, go online or follow on X, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and YouTube.

    What movies are available on MGM+?

    Kit Harington as Sydney Carton starring in 'A Tale of Two Cities.'
    The MGM+ service offers an expansive library of film titles from MGM and other major Hollywood studios. Browse the full selection for updates, which include crowd-pleasers released by MGM:
    • Nickel Boys
    • Regretting You
    • Wild Cat
    • Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning
    • Roofman
    • The Count of Monte Cristo
    • Blink Twice
    • Gladiator II

    What else can I watch on Prime Video?

    MGM+ is one of many Prime Video Subscriptions you can add to your account to watch thousands of shows and movies. Members can also stream programming from partners such as Apple TV, HBO Max, Crunchyroll, and Peacock Premium Plus as well as more than 900 free ad-supported (FAST) channels.
    There are also plenty of Amazon Original movies and series to watch on Prime Video, such as Reacher, Cross, The Summer I Turned Pretty, The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power, and Fallout.
    Next, learn how to use Prime Video's "Shop the Show" feature to discover products from your favorite series, movies, and live sports.
    This story has been updated with the newest information about MGM+ and its content.
